us.headline: Tariff stacks and origin rules — not mill prices — drive your landed cost.

regulation.b: China-origin goods carry additive Section 301 duties (many lists 25%; consumer List 4A 7.5%). USTR's second four-year review is underway — comment windows on the 2018 actions run through summer 2026 (the 23 Aug 2018 action window opens 24 Jun 2026) — and current product exclusions run only to 9 Nov 2026. Origin and HTS classification materially change landed cost; confirm each item's status before costing.
Duties compose additively (MFN + Section 301 + any Section 232 on steel/aluminium). Confirm each product's HTS code before costing.

us.supply: Asia–US (transpacific) spot rates are spiking on early peak-season demand, pre-tariff frontloading and 2026 FIFA World Cup cargo (Drewry reports rates to US West Coast at $6,482 per FEU and US East Coast at $7,904 per FEU) — booking space early matters more than chasing a dip. Brent crude surged to $90 USD per barrel on July 20, 2026, due to escalating US-Iran military tensions and the near-complete shutdown of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. This keeps resin and energy-linked input costs highly volatile, though polypropylene resin prices are softening.
us.view: US landed cost is dominated by tariffs and origin, not spot material prices. Birdland's two-country production (Taiwan + China) lets you shift origin to manage tariff exposure, and we prepare clear origin documentation. With transpacific freight now firming into peak season, the smart move is to lock capacity and contract rates early — our pricing reflects secured capacity and compliant origin, not a single day's index.

summary.changed: Transpacific freight rates are surging into peak season, and Brent crude has spiked to $90/bbl.
summary.action: Secure ocean capacity and contract rates immediately, and lock in fuel-linked inputs.

ca.headline: ‘Country of melt’ now decides whether China steel surtaxes hit you.

regulation.b: Since 31 Jul 2025 Canada applies a 25% surtax on steel melted & poured (and aluminium cast) in China, on top of normal duties. From 26 Dec 2025 a 25% tariff also covers listed steel-derivative products — some with only partial steel content. The surtax regime has been extended to June 27, 2027.

Several temporary remissions narrowed from 1 Jul 2026 — now in effect — including the end of relief for aluminium, public-health/safety goods, and motor-vehicle and aircraft inputs, though the Steel Derivative Goods Surtax Remission Order (Feb 2026; CBSA guidance Mar 2026) keeps category-based relief for a defined list, extended to June 30, 2027 for some auto and aerospace inputs.

ca.supply: Landed steel cost into Canada is shaped more by origin-of-melt surtaxes than by mill prices. Brent crude surged to $90 USD per barrel on July 20, 2026, due to escalating US-Iran military tensions and the near-complete shutdown of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. This keeps resin and energy-linked input costs highly volatile, though polypropylene resin prices are softening.
ca.view: Birdland can source Taiwan-melted steel content with clear traceability, helping you stay outside the China-melt surtax, and we document origin end-to-end. The driver here is provenance and paperwork, not spot steel — our quotes reflect verified origin and stable supply.

summary.changed: Canada's steel surtax regime is extended to June 2027, with Brent crude spiking to $90/bbl.
summary.action: Verify product classification and country of melt, and consider locking fuel-linked inputs.

au.headline: Timber biosecurity and legal-logging compliance gate every wood-handled tool.

regulation.b: Wood-handled goods must meet DAFF biosecurity conditions — bark-free, pest-free, treated/certified — checked against the BICON database.
The Illegal Logging Prohibition Act requires due diligence on timber, and wood packaging must meet ISPM 15. Phytosanitary certificates have been electronic (ePhyto) since Aug 2025.

au.supply: Compliance and treatment readiness, more than freight price, determine clearance speed into Australia. Brent crude surged to $90 USD per barrel on July 20, 2026, due to escalating US-Iran military tensions and the near-complete shutdown of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. au.view: Birdland supplies treated, certified, documented timber handles and ISPM 15-compliant packaging, so shipments clear cleanly. The value we sell is documented compliance and reliability — not the lowest spot timber cost.

summary.changed: Timber biosecurity remains critical, and Brent crude has surged to $90/bbl due to M.E. tensions.
summary.action: Ensure all wood-handled products have complete phytosanitary certificates and monitor energy-linked costs.

uk.headline: Britain is diverging from the EU — plan to GB rules separately.

Treat GB requirements separately from the EU.
UK product-marking and conformity rules still apply alongside EU rules for dual-market ranges — confirm marking per market.

Duties compose additively (MFN + Section 301 + any Section 232 on steel/aluminium). Confirm each product's HTS code before costing.

us.supply: Asia–US (transpacific) spot rates are spiking on early peak-season demand, pre-tariff frontloading and 2026 FIFA World Cup cargo (Drewry reports rates to US West Coast at $6,482 per FEU and US East Coast at $7,904 per FEU) — booking space early matters more than chasing a dip. This keeps resin and energy-linked input costs highly volatile, though polypropylene resin prices are softening.

With transpacific freight now firming into peak season, the smart move is to lock capacity and contract rates early — our pricing reflects secured capacity and compliant origin, not a single day's index.

ca.headline: ‘Country of melt’ now decides whether China steel surtaxes hit you.

From 26 Dec 2025 a 25% tariff also covers listed steel-derivative products — some with only partial steel content. The surtax regime has been extended to June 27, 2027.

Several temporary remissions narrowed from 1 Jul 2026 — now in effect — including the end of relief for aluminium, public-health/safety goods, and motor-vehicle and aircraft inputs, though the Steel Derivative Goods Surtax Remission Order (Feb 2026; CBSA guidance Mar 2026) keeps category-based relief for a defined list, extended to June 30, 2027 for some auto and aerospace inputs.

ca.supply: Landed steel cost into Canada is shaped more by origin-of-melt surtaxes than by mill prices. global.headline: Three forces shape 2026: tighter EU compliance, tariff/origin rules, and extreme freight + energy volatility.

regulation.b: The EU Deforestation Regulation applies from 30 Dec 2026 for large/medium operators (small & micro to 30 Jun 2027). regulation.t: CBAM — carbon cost on steel content
regulation.b: CBAM entered its definitive phase on 1 Jan 2026; financial obligations on embedded carbon in imported steel now apply, with the first certificate surrender in 2027. On 6 July 2026, the European Parliament's Environment Committee (ENVI) adopted a position to expand CBAM to selected downstream products and add anti-circumvention rules, aligning with the Council's June position. Steel-content items (blades, frames) face added landed cost and reporting. The Q2 2026 CBAM certificate price is EUR 75.28 per tonne.

US Section 301 duties stack additively and current exclusions run only to 9 Nov 2026 (with USTR weighing further tariff actions), while Canada's 25% surtax on China-melted steel means country of origin increasingly decides landed cost. Canada's steel surtax regime has been extended to June 27, 2027.

global.supply: Asia–Europe and transpacific freight are both firming sharply into peak season (Drewry WCI rose 2% to US$4,639/FEU on 09 July 2026, with Shanghai–LA up 2% to US$6,482/FEU; CMA CGM FAK rates for Asia to North Europe are $7,000 per FEU, effective July 15, 2026). Brent crude surged to $90 USD per barrel on July 20, 2026, due to escalating US-Iran military tensions and the near-complete shutdown of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The Strait of Hormuz remains under critical security threat with commercial traffic near-completely shut down, keeping resin and energy-linked input costs highly volatile, though polypropylene resin prices are softening.
global.view: Across every market the pattern is the same: compliance documentation and origin now matter more than spot prices. Birdland's two-country production, early freight booking, material locking and full compliance paperwork (EUDR, CBAM data, ISPM 15, origin) keep you a season ahead. Our quotes reflect secured materials, compliant documentation and reliable supply — when an index dips, that stability is exactly what protects your margin.

summary.changed: Global compliance (EUDR, CBAM) and origin rules are tightening, while freight and energy prices surge.
summary.action: Prioritize robust documentation for EU-bound shipments and secure ocean freight capacity early.

news.title: Birdland at spoga+gafa 2026 — Hall 04.2 / C011, Cologne, 22–24 June

teamAnalysis: Compliance and origin remain the primary drivers of landed cost into 2026, overshadowing spot prices. EU buyers face tightening EUDR, CBAM, and PPWR PFAS regulations, while US and Canadian costs are shaped by tariff stacks and country-of-origin/melt rules. Ocean freight has surged sharply on both Asia–Europe and transpacific lanes, driven by peak season demand and pre-tariff frontloading. Brent crude remains highly volatile, surging to $90 USD per barrel due to escalating US-Iran military tensions and the near-complete shutdown of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, though polypropylene resin prices continue to soften. These market dynamics reinforce the importance of securing supply, capacity, and robust documentation early. Birdland's two-country production model, proactive freight booking, strategic material locking, and comprehensive compliance paperwork ensure stable supply and protect your margins amidst ongoing volatility.

expert: It tracks recent and pending duty changes by market, including US Section 301 proposals and Canada's steel surtax.

Pair it with the landed-cost calculator to quantify each change before it hits a costing.

expert: Each event is scored 0–100 for pressure on oil, freight, transit time and insurance.

Birdland's two-country production buffers single-point shocks, keeping supply moving if one route is hit.

p-mkt.simple: Like reading two top weather stations (BNP Paribas & Citi), then telling you what to pack for your market.
expert: Cross-referencing publicly reported BNP Paribas and Citi research: Citi now anticipates Brent crude volatility due to Middle East tensions, with prices potentially remaining elevated around ~US$90/bbl in the near term before stabilizing around ~US$75-80/bbl into late 2026, impacting fuel- and freight-linked costs.

BNP Paribas flags tariff-reconfiguration risk (the US tariff court ruling, USMCA renegotiation) and China–EU trade tensions as key watch-items, alongside ongoing Red Sea and Strait of Hormuz disruptions.

Birdland's takeaway for your sourcing: prioritize locking supply and keeping origin flexible to navigate tariff rules and geopolitical risks, treating current price fluctuations as a call for stability.

advice.zh: 【Birdland 供應鏈週報】本週亞洲—歐洲與亞洲—美西海運運價均大幅走升,進入旺季高峰;鋼價因CBAM碳成本與需求支撐偏強,木材與塑膠粒則持續走軟。Brent原油因荷姆茲海峽緊張局勢飆升至90美元/桶。建議:歐洲線與美西線均應提前訂艙鎖運費,並分批提前鎖定鋼質料件與燃料相關投入。法規面 EUDR(2026/12/30)木柄文件請務必提早備齊,並留意PPWR(2026/8/12)對包裝PFAS的限制。需要我們協助規劃備料節奏與文件,隨時聯繫。
advice.en: [Birdland Supply-Chain Brief] Asia–Europe and transpacific ocean freight rates are both surging into peak season; steel stays firm due to CBAM carbon costs and demand, while lumber and resin continue to soften. Brent crude surged to 90 USD/bbl due to Strait of Hormuz escalation. We suggest booking all peak-season ocean space early to lock freight and phasing in steel-input and fuel-linked cover. On compliance, prepare EUDR (30 Dec 2026) timber documentation early and thoroughly, and note PPWR (12 Aug 2026) PFAS limits for packaging. Contact us to plan your buying cadence and paperwork.

materials_bot.simple: 原料像做菜的食材——鋼和包材在漲、木材和塑膠在跌,整體成本『一半漲一半跌』,不會單看一項就決定報價。
expert: 對台灣出口:鋼與電鍍/包材偏漲,鋼質手工具到岸成本壓力升,建議分批鎖鋼料、優化包材規格。
對中國出口:木材與樹膠走軟有利木柄/握把成本,但 EUDR(木材)合規文件成關鍵門檻。
綜合:成本結構分散,維持台灣+中國雙基地調度,用『鎖料+文件齊備』把波動轉成穩定供應的賣點。
